channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at Harvard's international students and tax-exempt status
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration has escalated its ongoing battle with Harvard, threatening to revoke the university's ability to host international students as the president called for withdrawing Harvard’s tax-exempt status.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, FATIMA HUSSEIN and ALIA WONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Suspect in custody in Florida State shooting, source tells AP
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A suspect has been taken into police custody and multiple victims were reported in a shooting Thursday at Florida State University, a person familiar with the matter told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Roblox issued subpoena for potentially exposing children to graphic, harmful content
item.description: The attorney general expressed concern over the ability of predatory adults to freely send messages to minors.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Drake complains about Kendrick Lamar's 'Not Like Us' Super Bowl performance in new defamation claims
item.description: Hip hop superstar Drake is now complaining about rival rapper Kendrick Lamar's performance of “Not Like Us” at this year's Super Bowl, adding it to his defamation lawsuit against Universal Music Group over the diss track and its allegations of pedophilia against Drake.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dozens more possible jurors are screened in Weinstein retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people are undergoing screening Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial. The panel started taking shape Wednesday, when five women and four men were picked for the redo of the landmark #MeToo-era trial.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with more than 1,000 fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina teenager, aspiring truck mechanic, killed on way to senior prom
item.description: A teen in North Carolina was killed on Saturday in a car crash after he initially wasn't going to the event. NC State Highway Patrol troopers said speed was a factor in the crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington State University instructor arrested for alleged assault of student with Trump hat
item.description: Jay Sani, a student at Washington State University, told police he was assaulted for wearing a red hat with a slogan from President Donald Trump's campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Burchett signals support for sending US criminals to El Salvador
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Wednesday defended President Donald Trump’s suggestion that U.S. citizens convicted of violent crimes be sent to Salvadoran prisons.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Obama, Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: Former President Barack Obama, Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and former Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Ben Carson are among the most liked politicians, according to new YouGov surveys.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family holds funeral for young mother, only to learn she's still alive
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their loved one, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state. Shanita Hopkins reached out to Nexstar's WROC with a very unusual situation.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, KTLA 5

item.title: Kamala Harris holds lead in hypothetical California governor race, poll shows
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris would enjoy a wide lead over the rest of the field if she were to enter the 2026 race for California governor, the results of a recent poll show.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Moderate Republicans draw red line on Medicaid cuts in Trump agenda bill
item.description: Vulnerable and moderate House Republicans are warning House GOP leaders that they will not support the “one big, beautiful bill” encompassing President Trump’s legislative agenda if it includes cuts to Medicaid benefits.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Judge denies motion to delay Menendez brothers' resentencing hearing
item.description: The resentencing hearing for Eric and Lyle Menendez is going forward Thursday morning despite a motion for delay filed by Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman. Hochman filed the motion Wednesday, saying he wants time for the court to review a parole assessment ordered by Gov.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: New York family told missing woman was found dead, only to learn she's living in Detroit
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their sister, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, Fox 5

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: “Harvard bending the knee to antisemitism — driven by its spineless leadership — fuels a cesspool of extremist riots and threatens our national security,” Noem said in the release.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: What is DHgate? Americans flock to Chinese app on tariff fears
item.description: A Chinese e-commerce platform known for its dupes of luxury brands has shot to #2 on the US Apple app store.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: New aerial video captures police, dive team response to fatal Hudson River helicopter crash
item.description: New York Police Department divers were captured on aerial video responding to the fatal Hudson River helicopter crash last week in Jersey City, New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mississippi River named 'most endangered' by conservation group, cites FEMA cuts
item.description: The organization said the federal government plays a key role in protecting the river and helping homeowners prepare for, and rebuild after, major flooding.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: 2 service members dead after vehicle crashes near New Mexico Border Patrol station
item.description: The third service member involved in the accident was still hospitalized in critical condition as of Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died of complications from diabetes, says NYC medical examiner
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died as a result of complications from diabetes, New York City's medical examiner said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tariffs will cost families nearly $5K per year, Kentucky governor says
item.description: "That's months upon months upon months of groceries. That's months upon months of rent. That might be your entire annual deductible if you've got private health insurance coverage," Gov. Andy Beshear said.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, NewsNation

item.title: Lawyers file wrongful death notice against Idaho city for family of boy shot by police
item.description: A California law firm notified the city of Pocatello, Idaho, on Wednesday of its intention to file a federal wrongful death lawsuit on the behalf of the family of Victor Perez, a 17-year-old boy with intellectual disabilities who was shot multiple times by four police officers.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Report: New York could save $1.3B by cutting packaging waste
item.description: Beyond Plastics released a report on the Packaging Reduction and Recycling Infrastructure Act that showed it could save New Yorkers $1.3 billion in 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Text scams cost consumers $470M in 2024: FTC
item.description: Most commonly, the FTC said these scams started with a text message, specifically ones about issues with a fictitious package delivery.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Trachtenberg's cause of death revealed
item.description: In February, police responded to a 911 call shortly after 8 a.m. at a 51-story luxury apartment tower in Manhattan’s Hell’s Kitchen neighborhood, where officers found Trachtenberg “unconscious and unresponsive,” according to an NYPD statement.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system may orbit two failed stars, scientists say
item.description: A new Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system may orbit two failed stars, scientists reported Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Attorney: ICE agents shatter car window during arrest
item.description: Federal immigration agents reportedly shattered a car window in New Bedford while detaining a Guatemalan man on Monday—despite his attorney saying they had the wrong person.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, after the institution said it would defy the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: IRS tax deadline extended in multiple states: What to know
item.description: Residents in all or parts of 13 states can file for an extension to allow disaster-affected areas more time to submit their taxes.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WGN 9

item.title: Family of wrongly deported man calls for his return
item.description: White House officials and the leader of El Salvador are defending their decision to *not* return a Maryland man wrongly deported to El Salvador last month despite a Supreme Court ruling calling for the U.S. to facilitate his return.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WGN 9

item.title: Former Colorado deputy gets 3 years in prison for fatally shooting man who called for help
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of killing a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help was sentenced Monday to three years in prison, the maximum sentence, by a judge who said the shooting was about power.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NBA's West spots set, with the Nuggets, Clippers and Timberwolves in and the Warriors play-in bound
item.description: The Los Angeles Clippers worked overtime to clinch a playoff spot — and send the Golden State Warriors to the NBA's play-in tournament.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5
